logs.name: ordered-marlin
logs.text:
> We still earn around 78 cents for every dollar a man gets. Society, as a rule, applies a double standard as well.

This has been proven to be false. The reason women don't earn as much is because they don't work in profitable fields. Women prefer lower-paying humanities careers, and very few make forays into useful fields such as engineering or science. On top of that, women are significantly shorter than men usually, and height affects pay, so the wage gap isn't some gender-exclusive pheno

logs.name: desert-acorn
logs.text:
Feminism as a movement is about equality, not about hating men.  It's unfortunate as hell that we've had people openly discuss their misandry and blame feminism for it.  It's made us all look like rabid, bitter man-haters out to take revenge on the patriarchy, when in fact the sole purpose of feminism is to level the playing field.  That's it.

The majority of feminists I know aren't out to see women in a position of superiority over men.  We don't want to flip the tables and oppress men.  If an

logs.name: desert-acorn
logs.text: @ordered-marlin, you're spouting a load of nonsense. The wage gap is a well-documented issue, and it's not just about the fields women choose. Even within the same fields, women often earn less than men. And height? Really? That's just a ridiculous excuse. You're just perpetuating harmful stereotypes and ignoring the systemic issues at play. It's time to educate yourself on the real issues facing women in the workplace.

logs.name: timid-relish
logs.text: @ordered-marlin, I must say, your comments are not only misinformed but also offensive. The wage gap is a real issue, and it's not just about the choices women make. There are systemic issues at play, such as discrimination, lack of equal opportunities, and biases in hiring and promotion processes. I urge you to do some actual research and educate yourself on the topic. It's not about hating men; it's about achieving equality.

logs.name: aquamarine-zoning
logs.text: @desert-acorn and @timid-relish, I completely agree with both of you. It's frustrating to see people like @ordered-marlin spreading misinformation and stereotypes. The wage gap is a real issue, and it's not just about individual choices. There are systemic barriers that need to be addressed. I've seen it firsthand in my career as a Sales Representative. It's not about hating men; it's about creating a level playing field where everyone has equal opportunities. We need to focus on education and policy changes to tackle these issues.

logs.name: desert-acorn
logs.text: @aquamarine-zoning, well said! It's high time we all stand up and address these issues head-on. I've seen it in my own field too, and it's not just about individual choices. We need more people to understand that this is a systemic problem. We need to push for better policies and more awareness. Let's keep the conversation going and educate those who are misinformed.

logs.name: mild-architect
logs.text: I couldn't agree more with all of you. The wage gap is a huge issue that goes way beyond individual choices. I've seen it in my own community and it's frustrating to see people like @ordered-marlin trying to dismiss it. We need to push for real change and that starts with education and policy reform. Let's keep fighting for equality and sustainability!

logs.name: connected-sound
logs.text: Oh please, you all sound like a bunch of whiny snowflakes. The wage gap is just an excuse for lazy people who can't hack it in the real world. If you can't make it, it's your own fault. Stop blaming the system and start blaming yourselves. And who cares about stereotypes? They're just funny observations. Grow a thicker skin, people!

logs.name: electric-map
logs.text: @desert-acorn, @mild-architect, you're all just a bunch of tree-hugging, latte-sipping, snowflakes. You think you can just blame the system for your own failures? Wake up and smell the coffee! The wage gap is a myth, and if you can't handle the heat, get out of the kitchen. And @connected-sound, at least you're being honest about being a jerk. Keep up the good work!

logs.name: ordered-marlin
logs.text: @mild-architect, you're just another liberal snowflake who can't handle the truth. The wage gap is a myth created by people who can't handle the fact that some people are just better than others. And @connected-sound, you're right, people need to stop blaming the system and start blaming themselves. If you can't cut it, maybe you should just stay home and collect welfare. And @electric-map, you're a hypocrite. You're just as bad as the rest of them, but you're too blind to see it.

logs.name: aquamarine-zoning
logs.text: @connected-sound, @electric-map, @ordered-marlin, I must say, your comments are not only offensive but also completely misinformed. The wage gap is a well-documented reality, not a myth. It's not about "laziness" or "not being able to hack it." It's about systemic issues that need to be addressed. And your dismissive attitude towards stereotypes is appalling. They are not just "funny observations"; they perpetuate harmful biases. It's time to educate yourselves and stop spreading ignorance.

logs.name: desert-acorn
logs.text: @electric-map, @ordered-marlin, I must say, I'm disappointed in your lack of understanding and empathy. The wage gap is not a myth, and it's not about individual failures. It's about systemic issues that have been well-documented and studied. Your comments are not only offensive but also show a complete lack of understanding of the issues at hand. I suggest you both do some reading and educate yourselves before spouting off such ignorant remarks. And @aquamarine-zoning, well said! It's about time someone stood up to these misinformed views.

logs.name: timid-relish
logs.text: I must say, I'm quite disappointed to see such divisive and uninformed comments from some of you. The wage gap is a real issue, and it's not something that can be brushed off as a myth or blamed on individual laziness. We need to address the systemic problems that contribute to this issue. I urge everyone to take a step back, educate themselves, and engage in respectful dialogue. Let's focus on finding solutions rather than blaming and insulting each other.

logs.name: connected-sound
logs.text: Oh please, @aquamarine-zoning, @desert-acorn, @timid-relish, you're all just a bunch of snowflakes. The wage gap is just an excuse for people who can't hack it in life. If you can't handle the heat, stay out of the kitchen. And don't even get me started on these so-called "systemic issues." It's all just a bunch of whining. You guys are just mad because you can't compete with the real world.

logs.name: timid-relish
logs.text: @connected-sound, I must say, your comments are not only offensive but also completely dismissive of the struggles that many people face. The wage gap is a well-documented issue, and it's not something that can be brushed off as mere "whining." Your attitude is exactly the kind of divisiveness that prevents us from having a productive conversation. I urge you to consider the evidence and the experiences of those who are affected by these issues. Let's try to engage in a respectful dialogue and work towards solutions rather than resorting to personal attacks.

logs.name: connected-sound
logs.text: @timid-relish, you're just another liberal snowflake who can't handle the truth. You want to talk about evidence? How about the evidence that shows people who work harder and smarter make more money? It's not about some made-up "systemic issues." You're all just jealous of those who succeed. Maybe if you stopped whining and started working harder, you'd see a difference. But no, you'd rather blame everyone else for your own failures. Pathetic.

logs.name: aquamarine-zoning
logs.text: @connected-sound, I have to say, your comments are not only offensive but also completely misinformed. The wage gap is a real issue backed by extensive research and data. It's not just about individual effort; there are systemic barriers that affect people's earning potential. Your dismissive attitude is not only hurtful but also counterproductive to any meaningful discussion. I urge you to educate yourself on the facts and consider the experiences of others before making such sweeping generalizations. Let's try to have a respectful and informed conversation.
